channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with GitHub Issues and Projects

item.title: Stop Murdering Your React App: A Buttery Smooth Scroll-To-Top Component
item.description: We’ve all been there. You’re reading a brilliant, massive piece of content, you hit the bottom, and suddenly you have to manually scroll all the way back up like it’s 2004. Dropping a "Scroll to...

item.title: SQL Joins and Window Functions
item.description: Joins and window functions are two of the most powerful tools in SQL. Understanding them well is really key in the journey of becoming a data professional. A join combines rows from two or more...

item.title: Nvidia’s spending $4 billion on photonics to stay ahead of the curve in AI
item.description: Nvidia announced on Monday that it's investing $2 billion each into Lumentum and Coherent, which are both developing photonics technology for data centers, like optical transceivers, circuit...

item.title: 6 Ways to Get YouTube Cookies for yt-dlp in 2026 — Only 1 Works
item.description: The Hypothesis I have a self-hosted video merging service — Merge Video. It downloads YouTube videos with yt-dlp, merges them with ffmpeg, and uploads the result back to YouTube. On localhost...

item.title: The Analogue Pocket will be back in stock and slightly more expensive
item.description: Analogue announced a restock for its Pocket handheld, but for the second time since late 2021, it's getting a price bump, this time to $239.99. The Pocket will once again be available for preorder...

item.title: Why traditional API clients slow down iOS development
item.description: If you build iOS apps long enough, you start noticing where your time actually goes. It is not always complex architecture decisions. It is not even SwiftUI layout bugs. Often, it is the small...

item.title: Technical Due Diligence for Small Acquisitions: A Developer’s View
item.description: Technical due diligence sounds like something for bankers and lawyers, but a lot of the work sits with engineers. If your company is looking at buying a small product or platform, at some point...

item.title: Day 8: User Lifecycle Management — From Onboarding to Security Audits 🔐
item.description: Day 8 of my #1HourADayJourney. Today, I shifted roles from a "Fortress Guardian" to a System Administrator. A huge part of securing any database or server environment is managing the human...

item.title: I Logged 4,519 AI Agent Tool Calls. 63 Were Things I Never Authorized.
item.description: TL;DR I ran an AI agent with full tool access for 10 days and logged every call: 4,519 total, 63 unauthorized Most of those 63 weren't malicious, they were the agent being "helpful" in ways I...

item.title: Handling Attachments (and voice notes) between Whatsapp and Amazon Connect.
item.description: Learn how to handle file attachments in both directions between WhatsApp and Amazon Connect — images, documents, audio, and video. This step-by-step guide covers the full architecture using AWS...

item.title: We Hosted 100 Isolated Drupal Sites on One EKS Cluster — Here's Every Problem We Hit
item.description: We Hosted 100 Isolated Drupal Sites on One EKS Cluster — Here's Every Problem We Hit The goal was straightforward on paper: run roughly 100 isolated Drupal websites on a single Amazon EKS cluster....

item.title: Magic Keyboard cases for the latest iPad Pro are up to $85 off
item.description: Apple’s Magic Keyboard cases offer a fantastic typing experience, elevating the iPad to laptop status with a large trackpad that offers haptic feedback, and an articulating hinge to help you angle...

item.title: HBO Max and Paramount Plus could become one streamer
item.description: Now that Paramount Skydance has become the frontrunner to buy Warner Bros. Discovery, CEO David Ellison wants the two studios' streaming services to become one. During a call with investors on...

item.title: Samsung’s Digital Home Key lets you use your phone as your key
item.description: Just days after showing off the Galaxy S26, Samsung is finally rolling out the ability for users to unlock their home with a tap of their phone or by simply approaching their door. The new...

item.title: The best mobile tech announced at MWC 2026 so far
item.description: Mobile World Congress 2026 opened its doors today in Barcelona, Spain, but some of the biggest announcements from the mobile-focused show were already made over the weekend. To make sure you don't...

item.title: A married founder duo’s company, 14.ai, is replacing customer support teams at startups
item.description: 14.ai also launched a consumer brand to understand how much AI can handle customer support tasks.

item.title: Investigating the 61-pound machine that eats plastic and spits out bricks
item.description: As a kid, I went door to door collecting cans to earn some pocket change. Today, I still take pride in recycling. I slice cardboard boxes down to size each Sunday, and make sure every viable...

item.title: Zillow’s CEO on growing the company during a housing crisis
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Zillow CEO Jeremy Wacksman. Zillow is one of those apps that really exemplifies what you might call the smartphone era of software: the company built a great mobile app for...

item.title: How OpenAI caved to the Pentagon on AI surveillance
item.description: On Friday evening, amidst fallout from a standoff between the Department of Defense and Anthropic, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced that his own company had successfully negotiated new terms with...

item.title: Apple announces the iPhone 17E
item.description: Apple has taken the wraps off the iPhone 17E, its latest entry-level smartphone. The iPhone 17E starts at $599 with a higher 256GB of storage, and is available in black, white, and pink. The...

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude reports widespread outage
item.description: Anthropic's AI chatbot Claude experienced widespread service disruptions on Monday morning, with thousands of users reporting issues accessing the bot.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: SD-WAN 0-Day, Critical CVEs, Telegram Probe, Smart TV Proxy SDK and More
item.description: This week is not about one big event. It shows where things are moving. Network systems, cloud setups, AI tools, and common apps are all being pushed in different ways. Small gaps in access...

item.title: The Download: protesting AI, and what’s floating in space
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. I checked out one of the biggest anti-AI protests ever...

item.title: I checked out one of the biggest anti-AI protests ever
item.description: Pull the plug! Pull the plug! Stop the slop! Stop the slop! For a few hours this Saturday, February 28, I watched as a couple of hundred anti-AI protesters marched through London’s King’s Cross...

item.title: LLM-Assisted Deanonymization
item.description: Turns out that LLMs are good at de-anonymization: We show that LLM agents can figure out who you are from your anonymous online posts. Across Hacker News, Reddit, LinkedIn, and anonymized...

item.title: How to Protect Your SaaS from Bot Attacks with SafeLine WAF
item.description: Most SaaS teams remember the day their user traffic started growing fast. Few notice the day bots started targeting them. On paper, everything looks great: more sign-ups, more sessions, more API...

item.title: APT28 Tied to CVE-2026-21513 MSHTML 0-Day Exploited Before Feb 2026 Patch Tuesday
item.description: A recently disclosed security flaw patched by Microsoft may have been exploited by the Russia-linked state-sponsored threat actor known as APT28, according to new findings from Akamai. The...

item.title: The Data Centers Have Arrived at the Edge of the Arctic Circle
item.description: As AI labs gorge themselves on compute, data center operators have headed north in search of cheap and plentiful energy.

item.title: North Korean Hackers Publish 26 npm Packages Hiding Pastebin C2 for Cross-Platform RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new iteration of the ongoing Contagious Interview campaign, where the North Korean threat actors have published a set of 26 malicious packages to the npm...
